sections.paragraph:
στραγγαλόω 1 aor. pass. ἐστραγγαλώθην; pf. pass. ptc. ἐστραγγαλωμένος Tob 2:3 BA (Philo Mech. 57, 42; Alex. Aphr., Probl. 1, 76 Ideler) strangle (Tob 2:3 BA), pass., w. intr. sense choke ITr 5:1.—DELG s.v. στράγξ.

aor. aor. = aorist

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

  1. pf. = perfect

ptc. ptc. = participle

BA BA = Biblical Archaeologist—List 6

Philo Philo Mech , III–II b.c.—List 5

intr. intr. = intransitive

ITr ITr = Ignatius to the Trallians—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 947
